title: Operational Planner
description: Own the short-term horizon — keep daily execution on track using ERP and S&OE processes, from order prioritisation and actuals capture through to exception management and feedback to planning.

keyAreas: How execution fits into the broader operating model and what it means to be the bridge between plan and reality
How long-term, mid-term, and short-term planning horizons connect — and what the operational planner receives from the levels above
Who is in the planning team and how the operational planner role connects to supply, demand, and commercial functions
Why data quality at the point of execution is critical — and what happens to the plan when actuals are poor
What ERP is, why it is the system of record for execution, and how it differs from planning software
Execution fundamentals: what moves from plan to action, and how orders, actuals, and exceptions are managed
The S&OE cadence: near-term monitoring, exception-based working, and the integrated review
Planning software data objects and the network model: understanding what the planning system computes and why it matters for execution
Planning logic: push/pull, safety stock, and how the system nets inventory against demand — the upstream logic behind the orders operational planners execute
ERP data model: how master and transactional data are structured and how they drive execution logic
How data flows into and out of ERP and back to planning software
RASCI accountability: your role in the execution handshake across planning, supply, and demand functions
Data types and quality: what makes execution data reliable enough to plan from
End-to-end system integration: how planning software, ERP, and the wider landscape connect
Daily execution: order prioritisation, real-time visibility, and maintaining execution discipline
Capturing actuals accurately and on time so planning has a reliable baseline to work from
Exception management: identifying, triaging, and resolving near-term disruptions
Running S&OE: demand and supply monitoring, the integrated review, and escalation triggers
Navigating ERP: creating orders, processing goods receipts and issues, and reading execution output
Applying planning policy in execution: service level priorities, allocation rules, and order release logic
Governance and escalation: when to surface execution exceptions and how to resolve them without disrupting the plan
KPI ownership: execution metrics, on-time delivery, and how short-term performance feeds S&OP review
Data governance in execution: ownership of transactional data quality and integration with ERP
